, State of California 
Memorandum 
To 
: File 
Date 
: Oct. 10, 1979 
--
From 
fAIR PO .. ~trpejCTlCES COMMISSION 
Dwigire \'.>iC'Kerson 
Subject: Disclosure of Loan Guarantors 
Tony Bennetti, Assistant City Attorney of San Jose, asked if a co-signer 
(guarantor) of a loan or line of credit is a source of income to an official 
for disclosure and disqualification purposes. 
After discussing the matter 
at the October 1, 1979 opinion request rreeting, I infonned Mr. Bennetti by 
telephone t..'Iat the guarantor would be considered a source of incorre to the 
official. 
The official should describe the loan as carning from the bank on his 
Staterrent of Economic Interests with a reference specifically naming the 
individual who co-signed (guaranteed) the loan. 
The value of the services 
provided to the official, for the purposes of disqualification, should be 
the difference between the costs of obtaining the loan and the cost of the 
loan if it had not been guaranteed. 
This advice confirms the staff's earlier position requlrlllg the reporting 
of guarantor of loans. 
See Sarah Ca:rreron f s :rr:erro to the file dated January 29, 1979. 
DED:nrw 


State of California 
Memorandum 
To 
: Opinion Request Meeting Participants 
Dote 
: Sept. 28, 1979 
From 
: 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
Dwight Dickerson 
Subject: 
A councilman has developed a relationship over time with 
a developer who on occasion has business before the Council. 
This developer has agreed to co-sign a business loan for the 
official from a bank. 
The amount of the loan or line of credit 
is in excess of $1,000. 
The question to be resolved is whether the developer who 
has co-signed the note is a source of income to the official 
for disqualification and disclosure purposes. 
If so, what 
is the value of the service (co-signing) provided to the 
official? 
DED:nrw

sections.question: A councilman has developed a relationship over time with a developer who on occasion has business before the Council. This developer has agreed to co-sign a business loan for the official from a bank. The amount of the loan or line of credit is in excess of $1,000. The question to be resolved is whether the developer who has co-signed the note is a source of income to the official for disqualification and disclosure purposes. If so, what is the value of the service (co-signing) provided to the official?
sections.conclusion: The guarantor would be considered a source of income to the official. The official should describe the loan as coming from the bank on his Statement of Economic Interests with a reference specifically naming the individual who co-signed (guaranteed) the loan. The value of the services provided to the official, for the purposes of disqualification, should be the difference between the costs of obtaining the loan and the cost of the loan if it had not been guaranteed.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a loan guarantor (co-signer) constitutes a source of income for disclosure and disqualification purposes, and if so, how to value the service provided.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Loan guarantors must be reported as sources of income with specific naming on the Statement of Economic Interests. The value of the guarantee for disqualification purposes equals the difference between the actual loan costs and what the costs would have been without the guarantee.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document consists of two related memoranda: an opinion request memo dated Sept. 28, 1979 and the FPPC's response dated Oct. 10, 1979. The response letter format is informal (memo style) rather than traditional advice letter format. Reference made to prior position in January 29, 1979 memo by Sarah Cameron.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: A councilman has developed a relationship over time with a developer who on occasion has business before the Council. This developer has agreed to co-sign a business loan for the official from a bank. The amount of the loan or line of credit is in excess of $1,000. The question to be resolved is whether the developer who has co-signed the note is a source of income to the official for disqualification and disclosure purposes. If so, what is the value of the service (co-signing) provided to the official?

CONCLUSION: The guarantor would be considered a source of income to the official. The official should describe the loan as coming from the bank on his Statement of Economic Interests with a reference specifically naming the individual who co-signed (guaranteed) the loan. The value of the services provided to the official, for the purposes of disqualification, should be the difference between the costs of obtaining the loan and the cost of the loan if it had not been guaranteed.

After discussing the matter at the October 1, 1979 opinion request rreeting, I infonned Mr. Bennetti by telephone t..'Iat the guarantor would be considered a source of incorre to the official. The official should describe the loan as carning from the bank on his Staterrent of Economic Interests with a reference specifically naming the individual who co-signed (guaranteed) the loan. The value of the services provided to the official, for the purposes of disqualification, should be the difference between the costs of obtaining the loan and the cost of the loan if it had not been guaranteed. This advice confirms the staff's earlier position requlrlllg the reporting of guarantor of loans. See Sarah Ca:rreron f s :rr:erro to the file dated January 29, 1979. DED:nrw State of California Memorandum To : Opinion Request Meeting Participants Dote : Sept. 28, 1979 From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION Dwight Dickerson Subject: A councilman has developed a relationship over time with a developer who on occasion has business before the Council. This developer has agreed to co-sign a business loan for the official from a bank. The amount of the loan or line of credit is in excess of $1,000. The question to be resolved is whether the developer who has co-signed the note is a source of income to the official for disqualification and disclosure purposes. If so, what is the value of the service (co-signing) provided to the official? DED:nrw
embedding.summary: FPPC advised that a loan guarantor (co-signer) is a source of income requiring disclosure and must be specifically named on financial disclosure statements. The value of the guarantee for disqualification purposes is the cost savings from having the loan guaranteed.
